title: 5 Reasons Why 28 Years Later: The Bone Temple Flopped At The Box Office
text: The divisive ending of the first film introduced Jack O'Connell's antagonist Jimmy Crystal and alienated casual viewers. While critics praised The Bone Temple, the wild ending of its predecessor apparently discouraged some audiences from returning. The sequel faced immediate challenges from Avatar: Fire and Ash (still at number one), plus horror alternatives like The Housemaid. Upcoming releases including Return to Silent Hill and Sam Raimi's Send Help will further pressure the film's performance. Releasing the sequel just seven months after the first installment proved premature. Industry analysts note that spacing out sequels by a year typically performs better, denying audiences adequate time between connected films. At $60+ million budgets, these horror films required substantial box office returns to profit. The trilogy represented an exceptional gamble for a studio needing franchise stability. Despite Cillian Murphy's Oscar-winning status and confirmed return, Sony underplayed his brief appearance in promotional materials, wasting a significant selling point for attracting mainstream audiences.
